title: Rootless 'podman start' fails with containers that are both privileged and have "host" networking
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

If I create a container with rootless `podman create --network host ...`, then `podman start ...` fails with:
```
unable to start container ...: error reading container (probably exited) json message: EOF
```

Here's the `podman inspect` output for the container in question:

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is a physical laptop running Fedora 28 Silverblue 28.20180820.0.

comments.body: I think it can be specific to Silverblue.  I'll install it and take a look

I am trying on a fresh installed and upgraded Silverblue:

```
$ rpm-ostree status
State: idle
AutomaticUpdates: disabled
Deployments:
● ostree://pirate-28:fedora/28/x86_64/workstation
                   Version: 28.20180821.0 (2018-08-22 00:06:31)
                    Commit: 872b05e1f772ae87ef7e4c8a47f27d85c71dbe9fb5d3df7aba4ffdd120e5627b
              GPGSignature: Valid signature by 128CF232A9371991C8A65695E08E7E629DB62FB1

  ostree://fedora-workstation:fedora/28/x86_64/workstation
                   Version: 28.1.1 (2018-04-25 05:39:47)
                    Commit: ef3d3262fe9c62d20e18637d656943a285530363060696ece175f2313683003d
              GPGSignature: Valid signature by 128CF232A9371991C8A65695E08E7E629DB62FB1

$ /usr/bin/podman create --name foo --net host -ti --rm busybox echo hi
$ $ /usr/bin/podman start foo
foo
```

Could you please share what command exactly you've used?  What happens if you disable SELinux?

Thanks for taking a look!

I have a script like this to create a container called `fedora-toolbox-rishi:28`:
```
#!/bin/sh

toolbox_container="fedora-toolbox-$USER:28"

base_toolbox_image="registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28"
toolbox_image="fedora-toolbox-$USER:28"
working_container_name="fedora-toolbox-$USER-working-container"

buildah images --noheading | grep --quiet $toolbox_image
if [[ $? -eq 1 ]]; then
  buildah from --name $working_container_name $base_toolbox_image
  buildah containers --noheading | grep --quiet $working_container_name
  [[ $? -eq 1 ]] && echo "Failed to create working container" && exit 1

  buildah run $working_container_name -- useradd \
      --no-create-home \
      --uid $UID \
      --groups wheel \
      $USER
  buildah config --volume $HOME $working_container_name
  buildah config --volume /run/user/$UID $working_container_name

  buildah config --user $USER $working_container_name
  buildah config --workingdir $HOME $working_container_name

  buildah commit --rm $working_container_name $toolbox_image
fi

podman create \
    --hostname toolbox \
    --interactive \
    --name $toolbox_container \
    --network host \
    --privileged \
    --security-opt label=disable \
    --tty \
    --volume $HOME:$HOME \
    --volume /run/user/$UID:/run/user/$UID \
    $toolbox_image
```

This is what happens when I used it to create the container and try to start it:
```
$ ./toolbox-create.foo 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6b8551f02cfd71ef7b00fc994a1c573c53409552504fc0edd4cc14164e2e40d9
 85.23 MiB / 85.23 MiB [====================================================] 6s
Copying config sha256:e555121ced0fcad9197d7d0445daff0e42d8f0e0c37362b66b817b8713dcbb3a
 1.27 KiB / 1.27 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
fedora-toolbox-rishi-working-container
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:85a307ac1d1c06039b5964988f4afd62a7a2dede8092e064f1e8c0d4c29d9de3
Copying blob sha256:6b50ad7e8b8f8d7602eedad7013b76b45bf5b6498c8bd9430a4c3c0fccb2e8c8
 1.92 KiB / 1.92 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:3d3ad3d1bde8221da6f58428e2f9f67818ab50b2813dbab4f5b6e7e7a49ffb0e
 644 B / 644 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3d3ad3d1bde8221da6f58428e2f9f67818ab50b2813dbab4f5b6e7e7a49ffb0e
043d4177025b5408fb57b1e2c06e6774bc35f2bc2e82e7831f6e5351fb98d142

$ podman start fedora-toolbox-rishi:28 
unable to start container "fedora-toolbox-rishi:28": error reading container (probably exited) json message: EOF
```

SELinux on my host OS is already in permissive:
```
$ getenforce 
Permissive
```

... and I believe I have disabled it in the above container too with `--security-opt label=disable`.
Looks like it's the `--privileged` flag that's causing the problem. I added it to make `ptrace` work inside the container. Is it not meant to work rootless?
thanks, the combination of `--privileged` and `--net host` makes it fail, I can reproduce now

Another thing that I have noticed, is that once I do manage to start it by dropping either `--privileged` or `--network host`, I can't stop it any more. `podman stop ...` fails with:
```
container_linux.go:377: signaling init process caused "operation not permitted"
failed to stop container 5eb2b4a3d08c2ead49fdde21ce8e00381cff0226b6927d7f657a9591679a6161: operation not permitted
```

But maybe we should use a different issue to discuss that.
Yes, PR #1323 fixed the problem for me.
